title: andropogon rufus subvar. approximatus
intro: Andropogon rufus subvar. approximatus, also known as Red Brome or Red Rescue Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Andropogon rufus subvar. approximatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally, depending on the intended application.
